questions.question: What is the CSS box model?
options: B) content + padding + border + margin — defines space element takes and how dimensions are calculated
questions.correctAnswer: B) content + padding + border + margin — defines space element takes and how dimensions are calculated

questions.question: What is the difference between box-sizing: content-box and border-box?
options: B) content-box: width=content only; border-box: width includes padding+border — border-box easier to work with
questions.correctAnswer: B) content-box: width=content only; border-box: width includes padding+border — border-box easier to work with

questions.question: What is CSS specificity?
options: B) Weight system determining which CSS rule applies — inline > ID > class > element
questions.correctAnswer: B) Weight system determining which CSS rule applies — inline > ID > class > element

questions.question: What is the difference between display:none and visibility:hidden?
options: B) display:none removes element from layout (no space); visibility:hidden hides but keeps space
questions.correctAnswer: B) display:none removes element from layout (no space); visibility:hidden hides but keeps space

questions.question: What is CSS Flexbox main purpose?
options: B) 1D layout system distributing space along single axis (row or column) with alignment control
questions.correctAnswer: B) 1D layout system distributing space along single axis (row or column) with alignment control

questions.question: What is the difference between justify-content and align-items in Flexbox?
options: B) justify-content aligns along main axis; align-items aligns along cross axis
C) align-items is for main axis
D) Both align on same axis
questions.correctAnswer: B) justify-content aligns along main axis; align-items aligns along cross axis

questions.question: What is CSS Grid vs Flexbox?
options: B) Grid is 2D (rows AND columns simultaneously); Flexbox is 1D (row OR column)
questions.correctAnswer: B) Grid is 2D (rows AND columns simultaneously); Flexbox is 1D (row OR column)

questions.question: What is a CSS pseudo-class?
options: B) Selector targeting element in specific state: :hover, :focus, :nth-child, :not()
questions.correctAnswer: B) Selector targeting element in specific state: :hover, :focus, :nth-child, :not()

questions.question: What is the difference between :before and ::before?
options: B) ::before is CSS3 syntax (preferred); :before is CSS2 (still works) — both insert content before element
questions.correctAnswer: B) ::before is CSS3 syntax (preferred); :before is CSS2 (still works) — both insert content before element

questions.question: What is CSS position:sticky?
options: B) Element scrolls normally until reaching threshold, then sticks in place relative to viewport
questions.correctAnswer: B) Element scrolls normally until reaching threshold, then sticks in place relative to viewport

questions.question: What is z-index and what does it require?
options: B) Controls stacking order — only works on positioned elements (position not static)
questions.correctAnswer: B) Controls stacking order — only works on positioned elements (position not static)

questions.question: What is a CSS custom property (variable)?
options: B) Variables defined with --name: value syntax, accessed with var(--name) — scoped to selector
questions.correctAnswer: B) Variables defined with --name: value syntax, accessed with var(--name) — scoped to selector

questions.question: What is CSS specificity order from highest to lowest?
options: B) inline styles > ID (#) > class/pseudo-class (.) > element tag
questions.correctAnswer: B) inline styles > ID (#) > class/pseudo-class (.) > element tag

questions.question: What is a media query?
options: B) CSS rule applying styles conditionally based on device characteristics like screen width
questions.correctAnswer: B) CSS rule applying styles conditionally based on device characteristics like screen width

questions.question: What is CSS transform vs transition?
options: B) transform changes element geometry (rotate/scale/translate); transition animates property change over time
questions.correctAnswer: B) transform changes element geometry (rotate/scale/translate); transition animates property change over time

questions.question: What is BEM naming convention?
options: B) Block__Element--Modifier — structured naming methodology preventing CSS conflicts
questions.correctAnswer: B) Block__Element--Modifier — structured naming methodology preventing CSS conflicts

questions.question: What is CSS inheritance?
options: B) Some CSS properties automatically passed from parent to children (color, font) unless overridden
questions.correctAnswer: B) Some CSS properties automatically passed from parent to children (color, font) unless overridden

questions.question: What is will-change property used for?
options: B) Hints browser about upcoming transform/opacity changes — allows GPU optimization in advance
questions.correctAnswer: B) Hints browser about upcoming transform/opacity changes — allows GPU optimization in advance

questions.question: What is the difference between em and rem?
options: B) em is relative to parent element's font-size; rem is relative to root (html) element's font-size
C) rem is relative to parent
questions.correctAnswer: B) em is relative to parent element's font-size; rem is relative to root (html) element's font-size

questions.question: What does contain:layout do in CSS?
options: B) Creates containment boundary — browser can optimize element independently without recalculating rest of page
questions.correctAnswer: B) Creates containment boundary — browser can optimize element independently without recalculating rest of page

questions.question: What does CSS stand for?
questions.correctAnswer: B) Cascading Style Sheets

questions.question: Which CSS property controls text size?
questions.correctAnswer: B) font-size
